Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your fleet system is accessible to drivers and managers anywhere, protects sensitive data under Singapore's PDPA rules, and connects seamlessly with your accounting and HR systems. SMEs need this foundation to operate efficiently across multiple devices and comply with data protection regulations. For example, a delivery company's drivers can log trips on their phones while the manager reviews costs in the office, and all data syncs securely to the accounting system.

Vehicle Registry.narrative: This family maintains a single source of truth for every vehicle in your fleet—from registration details to insurance policies and claims history. SMEs benefit by having all vehicle information in one place, reducing paperwork and speeding up insurance claims when accidents happen. For instance, when a vehicle is involved in an incident, you can instantly pull its insurance details and claims history to file a claim quickly.

Driver & Safety.narrative: This family tracks driver qualifications, monitors unsafe driving habits like speeding or harsh braking, and records accidents to reduce risk and insurance costs. SMEs should care because safer drivers mean fewer claims, lower premiums, and better compliance with employment and road safety rules. A logistics company can use safety scores to identify drivers needing retraining, preventing costly accidents and protecting their reputation.

Maintenance & Workshop.narrative: This family automates service reminders based on time or mileage and tracks all repairs and maintenance work orders in one place. SMEs benefit by avoiding costly breakdowns, extending vehicle life, and keeping maintenance costs predictable. For example, a transport operator can set reminders for every vehicle's 10,000 km service, ensuring no vehicle is overlooked and reducing unexpected downtime.

Operations & Logistics.narrative: This family tracks fuel consumption, vehicle location in real time, trip details, and vehicle availability for booking—giving you visibility and control over daily fleet operations. SMEs need this to spot fuel waste, monitor driver behaviour, and make sure shared vehicles are used efficiently. A property management company can use GPS tracking to confirm service teams are visiting sites on time, and fuel tracking to catch unusual consumption patterns.

Cost & Compliance.narrative: This family calculates the true cost of owning each vehicle, generates reports on fleet performance, and tracks regulatory obligations like road tax and vehicle inspections. SMEs should care because understanding total cost per vehicle helps with budgeting and replacement decisions, while compliance tracking prevents fines and operational disruptions. A construction company can use cost reports to decide whether to replace an ageing truck, and compliance alerts to ensure all vehicles pass their annual inspections on time.
